Appearance
From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

2013 Africa Cup of Nations squads
← 2012
2015 →
International football competition

The 2013 Africa Cup of Nations was an international football tournament held in South Africa from 19 January until 10 February 2013.[1]

The Confederation of African Football confirmed 9 January as the deadline for final squad submissions ahead of the tournament. The African confederation wanted all 23-man squad lists sent to its Cairo headquarters by midnight GMT on 9 January. Failing to do so would result in a fine and infringing countries will only be allowed to take a 22-man squad to the finals.

Along with their 23-man squads, countries were allowed to bring a delegation of 17 officials.[2]

Algeria

[edit]

Coach: Vahid Halilhodžić

A 40-man provisional squad was announced on 10 December 2012.[20] A 24-man list was announced on 18 December 2012, with Ishak Belfodil, Mokhtar Benmoussa, Antar Boucherit, Madjid Bougherra, Hamza Boulemdaïs, Ismaël Bouzid, Farouk Chafaï, Abdelmoumene Djabou, Moustapha Djallit, Rafik Djebbour, Ahmed Gasmi, Abderahmane Hachoud, Féthi Harek, Hamza Koudri and Lamine Zemmamouche being left out of the team.[21] On 22 December 2012, Djamel Abdoun dropped out of the squad after an injury, thus leaving the team with a final 23-man squad.[22]

No. Pos. Player Date of birth (age) Caps Goals Club
1 1GK Azzedine Doukha (1986-08-05)5 August 1986 (aged 26) 2 0 USM El Harrach
2 3MF Mehdi Mostefa (1983-08-30)30 August 1983 (aged 29) 10 0 Ajaccio
3 2DF Liassine Cadamuro (1988-03-05)5 March 1988 (aged 24) 4 0 Real Sociedad
4 2DF Essaïd Belkalem (1989-01-01)1 January 1989 (aged 24) 4 0 JS Kabylie
5 2DF Rafik Halliche (1986-09-02)2 September 1986 (aged 26) 22 1 Académica de Coimbra
6 2DF Djamel Mesbah (1984-10-09)9 October 1984 (aged 28) 16 0 Milan
7 3MF Ryad Boudebouz (1990-02-19)19 February 1990 (aged 22) 15 1 Sochaux

References

[edit]
  1. ^ "SA to host 2013 African Cup of Nations, a boost to hospitality industry". Archived from the original on 27 October 2012. Retrieved 28 October 2012.
  2. ^ CAF Sets Deadline For Submission Of 2013 AFCON Team List Deprecated link archived 15 April 2013 at archive.today Osun Defender. Retrieved 15 December 2012
  3. ^ Gordon Igesund announces 2013 african cup of nations squad Archived 2 February 2013 at the Wayback Machine Kickoff.com. Retrieved 30 December 2012
  4. ^ [1] Kickoff.com. Retrieved 12 January 2012
  5. ^ Cape Verde name 2013 Africa Cup of Nations squad Goal.com. Retrieved 19 December 2012
  6. ^ "Nations Cup 2013: Cape Verde name squad". BBC Sport. 18 December 2012.
  7. ^ AFCON 2013: Angola announce their 23 man final squad Archived 13 January 2013 at the Wayback Machine Kawowo. Retrieved 9 January 2013
  8. ^ Morocco unveil squad for Afcon 2013 Goal.com. Retrieved 10 January 2013
  9. ^ Breaking News: Sumaila dropped from final Ghana squad for 2013 AFCON Archived 19 January 2013 at the Wayback Machine MyJoyOnline. Retrieved 9 January 2013
  10. ^ Paris Saint Germain midfielder Momo Sissoko makes Mali Afcon squad Goal.com. Retrieved 10 January 2013
  11. ^ "MTN Football Page has moved". Archived from the original on 4 March 2016. Retrieved 8 January 2013. MTN Football. Retrieved 7 January 2013
  12. ^ DR Congo publish Afcon 2013 squad Goal.com. Retrieved 9 January 2012
  13. ^ Renard names AFCON squad Times of Zambia. Retrieved 24 December 2012
  14. ^ Nigeria announce final Afcon squad of 23 players Kawowo. Retrieved 10 January 2013
  15. ^ AFCON 2013: Stallions name squad[permanent dead link] Ghanamma. Retrieved 8 January 2013
  16. ^ AFCON 2013: Stallions name squad Archived 17 January 2013 at the Wayback Machine Myjoyonline. Retrieved 8 January 2013
  17. ^ Champions St.George dominate Ethiopia AFCON squad
  18. ^ Cote D'Ivoire name regular squad for 2013 Afcon Goal.com. Retrieved 28 December 2012
  19. ^ Tunisia unveil Afcon squad Goal.com. Retrieved 10 January 2013
  20. ^ "CAN 2013 : Les 40 présélectionnés" (in French). dzfoot.com. 10 December 2012. Retrieved 16 October 2017.
  21. ^ "CAN 2013 : Les 23+1 convoqués pour la phase finale" (in French). dzfoot.com. 18 December 2012. Retrieved 16 October 2017.
  22. ^ "CAN 2013 : Djamel Abdoun blessé, déclare forfait" (in French). dzfoot.com. 22 December 2017. Retrieved 16 October 2017.
  23. ^ Adebayor in as Togo release final Afcon squad Goal.com. Retrieved 10 January 2013

  • Music
  • Trophy
Notes
The 2021 and 2023 tournaments were actually held in 2022 and 2024 respectively.
There were no 1957 and 1959 qualifications as places were given by invitation only.
The "finals" articles for 1959 and 1976 are about the decisive matches of final group stages.
Football in Africa portal
